situation: You are renting an apartment and the heating has not been working for a week. Write an email to your landlord.
points: Describe the problem with the heating
Explain how it is affecting you
Request that the issue be fixed by a specific date

situation: You borrowed a book from your neighbour two months ago and you accidentally damaged it. Write an email to your neighbour.
points: Apologize for the damage
Explain what happened
Suggest how you will make it up to them

situation: You recently attended a community event and were disappointed by the organization. Write an email to the event organizer.
points: Describe what the event was and when you attended
Explain what you were disappointed by
Offer a suggestion for improvement

situation: You need to take an unexpected week off work due to a family emergency. Write an email to your manager.
points: Explain that you need time off and when
Describe the situation briefly
Suggest how your work can be covered while you are away

situation: You ordered office supplies for your team two weeks ago, but the order has not arrived. Write an email to the supplier.
points: Provide the order details
Explain the inconvenience this has caused
Ask for an update and a delivery timeline

situation: You recently stayed at a hotel and were very satisfied with your experience. Write an email to the hotel manager.
points: Mention when you stayed and in which room type
Describe one or two things that impressed you
Say that you will recommend the hotel to others

situation: You bought a laptop online two weeks ago, but it stopped working after five days. Write an email to the company's customer service department.
points: Describe the product and when you bought it
Explain the problem you experienced
Request a refund or replacement

situation: A construction project near your home has been causing noise early in the morning, before the allowed hours. Write an email to the city bylaw enforcement office.
points: Describe the location and nature of the disturbance
Mention the times the noise occurs
Request that the rules be enforced

situation: You want to apply for a grant to start a community garden in your neighbourhood. Write an email to the city parks department.
points: Describe your idea for the community garden
Explain the benefits for the neighbourhood
Ask about the grant application process

situation: You are a student and you need an extension on a major assignment due to illness. Write an email to your professor.
points: Explain that you have been unwell
Request a specific extension (e.g., one week)
Assure your professor you will submit quality work by the new date

situation: A local survey asked residents whether the city should convert an unused parking lot downtown into a public park.
points: Option A: Keep the parking lot to support local businesses
Option B: Convert it into a public green space for residents

situation: A company survey is asking employees for their preference on work arrangements.
points: Option A: Work entirely from home
Option B: Work in the office full time

situation: A school board survey asked parents whether homework should be required for elementary school students.
points: Option A: Assign homework regularly to build study habits
Option B: Eliminate homework to reduce stress and allow more family time

situation: A national survey is asking whether voting in elections should be made mandatory for all citizens.
points: Your task: Do you support or oppose mandatory voting?
Use specific reasons and examples to explain your position.

situation: A city survey asked residents whether all public transit should be made free for everyone, funded by increased municipal taxes.
points: Your task: Do you support or oppose free public transit?
Use specific reasons and examples to explain your view.

situation: A consumer survey is asking whether stores should be required to charge an extra fee for plastic bags.
points: Your task: Do you support or oppose a mandatory plastic bag fee?
Give specific reasons and examples to support your position.

situation: A survey asked people whether smartphones should be banned in classrooms from kindergarten to Grade 12.
points: Your task: Do you support or oppose banning smartphones in schools?
Use specific reasons and examples to support your view.

situation: A government survey is asking whether all new residential buildings should be required to install solar panels.
points: Your task: Do you support or oppose this requirement?
Give specific reasons and examples.

situation: A survey is asking whether the government should raise the minimum wage significantly over the next two years.
points: Your task: Do you support or oppose raising the minimum wage?
Use specific reasons and examples to explain your position.

situation: A university is considering requiring all first-year students to take a mandatory course in financial literacy.
points: Your task: Do you support or oppose this requirement?
Use specific reasons and examples.
